Container.Name: khord
Container.Overview:
Khord is a self-hosted music sharing app. Share a song once — everyone listens on whichever streaming service they use (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Deezer, and more). Setlists, upvoting, cross-platform links, and an admin panel all included. All data stored in a single SQLite database — no external services required.
No reverse proxy is included — point your existing Unraid reverse proxy (NGINX Proxy Manager, Swag, Traefik, etc.) at port 3000 on this container. HTTPS is required for a publicly accessible instance.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port exposed by the container. Point your reverse proxy (NGINX Proxy Manager, Swag, etc.) at this port — do not expose it directly to the internet.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: Directory where the SQLite database and thumbnail cache are stored.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Publicly accessible URL for this instance, e.g. https://khord.yourdomain.com.

Config.Name: Owner Emails
Config.Description: Comma-separated email addresses granted admin/owner privileges. The first account registered with a matching email receives the admin role automatically.

Config.Name: Spotify Client ID
Config.Description: Spotify app client ID from developer.spotify.com. Optional — enables Spotify URL resolution and playlist import. Both Client ID and Client Secret are required together. After setting, enable Spotify in the admin panel Settings tab.

Config.Name: Spotify Client Secret
Config.Description: Spotify app client secret from developer.spotify.com. Required alongside Spotify Client ID. Keep this private. Note: the account that owns the Spotify app must have an active Spotify Premium subscription.

Config.Name: YouTube API Key
Config.Description: YouTube Data API v3 key from console.cloud.google.com. Optional — enables YouTube Music URL resolution when sharing songs. Also enable YouTube Music in the admin panel Settings tab. Note: free quota is ~100 searches/day (100 units per call, 10,000/day limit).

Config.Name: Apple Music Developer Token
Config.Description: MusicKit JWT from developer.apple.com → Keys. Optional — enables Apple Music playlist import. Requires an Apple Developer account ($99/yr). Tokens are valid up to 6 months; regenerate before expiry. After setting, enable in the instance admin panel under Settings.

Config.Name: Max Users
Config.Description: Maximum number of registered users. 0 = unlimited. Can also be adjusted live from the /admin panel.

Config.Name: App Name
Config.Description: Display name shown in the UI and page titles.

Config.Name: App Tagline
Config.Description: One-line tagline shown on the home page.

Config.Name: Disable Album Art
Config.Description: Set to true to hide album art thumbnails on song cards. Can also be toggled live from the /admin panel.

Config.Name: Theme
Config.Description: UI color theme. Requires a container restart to apply. Options — neutral dark: dark, zinc, slate, gray, neutral, stone. Neutral light: light, zinc-light, slate-light, neutral-light, stone-light. Chromatic dark: navy, teal, emerald, rose, violet.

Config.Name: Database File Name
Config.Description: File name of the SQLite database stored in the Data Path directory. Only change this if you need to rename the database file.

Config.Name: Thumbnail Cache Directory
Config.Description: Directory for the server-side album art disk cache. Lives inside the Data Path volume by default. Manage cache size from the admin panel (Admin → Cache tab).
